title: No TLS/mTLS in Transporters
description: All Transporters (TCP,UDS,WebSocket,Subprocess) send plain text without encryption — feature required for Gateway Mode and inter-container traffic
impact: All traffic between services is exposed — Gateway Mode cannot be used safely; even WebSocket does not truly support wss:// with TLS
dependencies: TRN-001 requires TLS for secure inter-container traffic
proposed_solution: Add ssl_context parameter to BaseTransporter.__init__() and modify TCPTransporter, UDSTransporter, WebSocketTransporter to use asyncio.start_server/TLS with wrap_socket
evidence: wiring/factories/transport_provider.py:55-60 does not pass ssl_context; transport/tcp.py:64-65 asyncio.open_connection() without ssl_context; transport/websocket.py:63-64 same issue
notes: All transport types need TLS support

title: No Transport-Level Backpressure
description: No mechanism to limit sending rate when receiver is congested — buffer may overflow in memory during heavy load especially with layered composition (Composite -> multiple children)
impact: OOM risk under heavy load — especially with SSE/streaming and Composite BROADCAST (all channels receive simultaneously)
proposed_solution: Add BackpressureStrategy to TransportConfig with throttle/callback/pause-resume based on buffer_size or ack latency; apply limits on asyncio.Queue in _merge_parallel_streams
evidence: No backpressure in transport/base.py or transport/config.py

title: No Keep-Alive / Heartbeat for TCP/UDS (WS has it)
description: WSKeepAlivePolicy exists in _ws_keepalive.py for WebSocketTransporter — but TCPTransporter and UDSTransporter have no heartbeat mechanism; long-lived TCP connections may be silently closed by kernel/proxy
impact: long-lived TCP and UDS connections may drop silently without detection
proposed_solution: Add setsockopt to enable TCP_KEEPALIVE + TCP_KEEPIDLE/TCP_KEEPINTVL or add optional HeartbeatConfig to BaseTransporter; UDS does not have kernel-level keepalive — needs special solution (simple ping frames)
evidence: transport/_ws_keepalive.py exists but not used for TCP/UDS; not tested anywhere

title: No Zero-Copy Path for Large Payloads
description: Every byte passes through serialize->compress->channel and back — no memory-mapped or file descriptor passing for large payloads (>100MB) causes GC pressure especially with Composite BROADCAST that replicates payload across N channels
impact: Files larger than 100MB consume duplicate memory and cause GC pressure
proposed_solution: Add optional LargePayloadHandler that detects >threshold and reads/writes from temporary file or mmap instead of RAM; pass file descriptor via UDS as immediate solution for same-device
evidence: transport/uds.py:87 uses writer.write(payload + b\\n\\) which is simple but not zero-copy"

title: Transporter Implementations Not Tested
description: TCPTransporter, UDSTransporter, InProcessTransporter, SubprocessTransporter, WebSocketTransporter — imports succeed but send()/stream()/serve()/close() functions have never been tested
impact: High risk of runtime errors on first real use — especially WebSocketTransporter which has structural bugs
proposed_solution: Create unit tests for each Transporter with mock socket/asyncio streams; test connect,fail,retry,close for each type; test WebSocketTransporter with HTTP upgrade mocking
evidence: transport/tcp.py and transport/uds.py and transport/websocket.py and others
notes: No test files exist for any transporter

title: CompositeTransporter Not Tested (but wired)
description: CompositeTransporter IS wired in factory.py via _build_composite() — creates it with children correctly and supports all strategies. But it is untested and not used in any real pipeline
impact: Cannot use multiple transport strategies without testing composite logic; risk lower because code appears structurally correct
proposed_solution: Write test to verify CompositeTransporter correctly routes to sub-transporters and isolates errors; test FALLBACK/PARALLEL/BROADCAST strategies
evidence: transport/composite.py exists and used in factory.py but without tests
notes: Lower priority but still needed for reliability

title: RetryEngine IS Wired to All Transporters (gap CLOSED)
description: RetryEngine is fully wired to all Transporters: TCP/UDS/InProcess/Subprocess/WebSocket all check self.retry_engine in send() and stream() and it is passed via __init__; factory always creates RetryEngine and passes it. This gap is closed — see TRN-009, TRN-010, TRN-011 for real breakable bugs
impact: Confirmed: every transporter uses retry_engine if available; factory always passes it
proposed_solution: No changes required — issue already resolved
evidence: transport/tcp.py:128-133, tcp.py:138-145; retry.py complete and wired; build_retry_decision_hook stub exists but not used

title: TransportConfig Fields Not All Used (ContextDefaults unused)
description: ContextDefaultsConfig (default_ttl_seconds, idle_timeout_seconds, offset_granularity, metadata_template) are not used anywhere — not passed to transporter, not passed to DeliveryReport, not passed to Channel. Also channel.headers and channel.max_message_size and channel.subprotocols are unused in transporters
impact: Transport YAML config does not actually affect behavior for context defaults; can configure WS options that are not applied
proposed_solution: Review TransportConfig and all transporter constructors to ensure all fields are passed; either use ContextDefaults when building TransportContext or remove them if unnecessary
evidence: transport/config.py contains the fields; transport/factory.py does not use them

title: WebSocketTransporter EXISTS but Has CRITICAL BUGS
description: WebSocketTransporter exists in transport/websocket.py with WSFramingEngine and WSKeepAlivePolicy — but contains structural bugs making it unrunnable: (1) _ensure_connection() has indentation error making except inside try block, (2) dead code lines 70-72 never executes, (3) dangerous recursiveness line 73 may cause infinite recursion, (4) serve() uses TCP start_server without HTTP upgrade handshake — not WS-protocol compatible
impact: WebSocketTransporter will fail at runtime on first attempt; serve() crashes and client waits forever for HTTP upgrade
proposed_solution: Rewrite _ensure_connection() to fix indentation and remove recursion; implement HTTP upgrade handshake in _handle_client before reading WS frames; add basic tests
evidence: transport/websocket.py lines 59-73 invalid for runtime; transport/__init__.py does not export WebSocketTransporter despite existence

title: InProcessTransporter TransportError Constructor Bug
description: All `raise TransportError(...)` calls in inprocess.py pass `type(self).__name__` (string) as first argument — but TransportError expects ErrorType enum as first argument. This causes TypeError at runtime on any InProcessTransporter error
impact: Any error in InProcessTransporter causes TypeError instead of classified TransportError — breaks InProcessTransporter completely
proposed_solution: Fix all TransportError calls in inprocess.py to pass ErrorType.TRANSIENT or ErrorType.PERMANENT instead of type(self).__name__
evidence: transport/inprocess.py lines 48-52, 65-69, 90-94, 97-102, 107-111, 128-131, 148-150, 161-163 — all contain same bug

title: SubprocessTransporter Missing serve() for INBOUND
description: SubprocessTransporter does not implement serve() — no INBOUND direction support via subprocess. Base class raises NotImplementedError.
impact: Cannot create Subprocess as inbound server — limits CLI as receive service
proposed_solution: Add serve() to SubprocessTransporter that reads from stdin continuously and returns responses via stdout — process-per-request or process-pool with keep-alive pattern
evidence: serve() exists in TCP/UDS/InProcess/WebSocket/Composite — Subprocess only one missing support

title: WebSocketTransporter Not Exported from __init__.py
description: transport/__init__.py does not export WebSocketTransporter nor WSKeepAlivePolicy nor WSFramingEngine — despite existing in websocket.py. Factory imports them directly (inside function)
impact: Users cannot import WebSocketTransporter directly — IDE discovery broken
proposed_solution: Add WebSocketTransporter and WSKeepAlivePolicy and WSFramingEngine to __init__.py and __all__

title: UDS Newline-Delimited Framing Fragile for Binary
description: UDS uses newline-delimited framing (b\\n) to delimit messages — any payload containing \\n within data will truncate the message. TCP uses length-prefixed safely while UDS does not
impact: Binary payloads cannot be transferred safely via UDS — silent errors may cause corrupted messages
proposed_solution: Change UDS to length-prefixed framing (like TCP: struct.pack + length header) with backward compatibility or explicit framing config
evidence: transport/uds.py:87 uses writer.write(payload + b\\n\\) which fails on binary \\n"

title: Channel.open() Accesses Private _ensure_connection
description: Channel.open() calls transporter._ensure_connection() directly — this breaks encapsulation and makes Channel tied to private method existing in each Transporter (not part of BaseTransporter interface)
impact: If a new Transporter is added without _ensure_connection, Channel.open() fails silently without opening the channel
proposed_solution: Add open() method to BaseTransporter (or async __aenter__) called by Channel instead of private method

title: ContextDefaultsConfig Fields Unused
description: ContextDefaultsConfig (default_ttl, idle_timeout, offset_granularity, metadata_template) defined in config.py and loaded from YAML — but not used in any: TransportContext, DeliveryReport, factory, or transporter. offset_granularity specifically should affect stream_offset in reports
impact: context_defaults config in YAML does not affect transport behavior — users think they configure session behavior but they don't
proposed_solution: Either link ContextDefaultsConfig to TransportContext when created (e.g. factory creates context with default values) or remove unused fields and document them
evidence: transport/config.py:49-59 defines ContextDefaultsConfig; transport/factory.py does not use it

title: CompositeTransporter Direction Hardcoded to OUTBOUND
description: CompositeTransporter.__init__ calls super().__init__(direction=Direction.OUTBOUND) without direction parameter — cannot create Composite with INBOUND directly
impact: In rare Composite INBOUND scenario, cannot use it — can workaround via adding direction to super()
proposed_solution: Add direction parameter to CompositeTransporter.__init__ and pass to super()
